artifacts.content:
Emily,

Kids are finally down and Duke’s snoring, so I figured I’d actually write this out instead of mumbling half of it while we’re passing in the hallway at 6 a.m.

Work’s been extra weird since that OSHA follow-up in the Truck Sectioning bay last month. HR put me on the hook to run that remedial safety training on March 12, 2025, in the conference room because of Miguel’s hand getting sliced on that rushed Ram 1500 cut. I get it, I was the one who pushed to hurry it along, but it still feels strange being “the example.” For the next Ram 1500 Quad Cab we process, I already told the yard guys to follow the cut map exactly like we laid it out on the whiteboard and to leave 4–5 inches of extra material around the B‑pillar and rocker so I can demo the right way to do it.

On top of that, Dad’s knees are worse and he’s got that oncology appointment at MercyOne Waterloo Medical Center on February 27, 2025 at 9:30 a.m. I’m planning to take him so Mom doesn’t have to drive. I’m trying to schedule that 2018 Ram 1500 Quad Cab sectioning job for later that afternoon so I can stay close to home in case his chemo hits him hard again and Laura needs me to swing back over to their place after I drop him off.

Meanwhile, normal life keeps rolling: Caleb’s basketball practice, Nora’s piano on Thursdays, that furnace bill still glaring at us from the counter. Some nights I end up scrolling used fishing boats after everyone’s asleep, then remember we’re just trying to make it to the Wisconsin Dells this summer.

Anyway, just wanted you to know what’s swirling in my head so it doesn’t all come out sideways when I’m tired.

Jeremy

metadata.subject: Work stuff, Dad’s appointment, and everything else

artifacts.content:
Shop reminder – Ram 1500 / training / back / parents

1) OSHA / Miguel / training
- Next clean 2013–2018 Ram 1500 Quad Cab that comes in and is straight on the right side: use it for the training cut.
- Follow the cut map we sketched on the whiteboard in the Truck Sectioning bay after the OSHA follow-up.
- When cutting through the right B‑pillar and rocker, tell the dismantlers: leave at least 4–5 inches of extra material beyond my lines so I can walk the new hires through the correct sectioning procedure.
- Remind myself: remedial safety training HR scheduled for March 12, 2025 – conference room. Miguel’s hand injury is still in my head; don’t rush them.

2) Dad / MercyOne / scheduling
- Feb 27, 2025 – 9:30 a.m. oncology appt for Dad (Richard Hansen) at MercyOne Waterloo Medical Center.
- Try to book the 2018 Ram 1500 Quad Cab right rear cab corner/upper B‑pillar sectioning for that afternoon only.
- Stay close to home so if chemo hits him hard again and Mom (Laura) calls from W 8th Street, I can bail out early.

3) Back / cab handling
- March 3, 2025 – 6:45 a.m. Allen Hospital, lower back check with Dr. Stevens.
- Tell yard: for that Ram right rear cab panel section, have them cut the rear cab panel a bit taller (down to just below midpoint of rear glass opening) and band it solid to the pallet so I can grab it solo with the forklift when I roll in late.

4) Personal
- Don’t be a hero with full cabs anymore. Ask for help.
- Long-term: start reading up on estimating software when kids are in bed.

metadata.title: Work + family juggling notes
metadata.location: Waterloo Auto Parts – Recycled Truck Department office

artifacts.content:
Event: Dad oncology appointment – MercyOne
Date: Thursday, February 27, 2025
Time: 9:30–11:30 a.m. (block extra time)
Location: MercyOne Waterloo Medical Center – oncology clinic
Attendees: Jeremy, Dad (Richard Hansen)

Notes:
- Pick Dad up from his place on the east side by 8:45 a.m.
- Bring list of meds, water bottle, light snack for him.
- Check with Mom (Laura) the night before about how he handled last chemo round; plan to stay with them for a bit afterward if he’s wobbly.
- Text Emily from the waiting room with an update so she can adjust kid pickup if needed.

Event: Ram 1500 Quad Cab – right side sectioning afternoon
Date: Thursday, February 27, 2025
Time: 1:30–4:30 p.m.
Location: Truck Sectioning bay – Waterloo Auto Parts
Attendees: Jeremy

Notes:
- Oversized cut requested from yard: right rear cab corner + upper B‑pillar area, leave cuts a little oversized and keep factory spot‑weld flanges intact.
- Idea is to section this 2018 Ram 1500 Quad Cab after getting back from MercyOne so I can stay close to home in case chemo hits Dad hard and Mom needs me back at W 8th Street.
- Keep phone on loud; if Laura calls, shut down early.
- Also keep in mind upcoming March 12 remedial safety training in the conference room tied to Miguel’s injury and OSHA follow-up – this Ram job is good practice for walking the guys through slower, cleaner cuts.

Dan,

Hope things are going alright over at Hawkeye. Hard to believe it’s been this long since I was in the collision lab sweating over that first frame pull.

I wanted to give you a heads-up that I’ve got a good candidate lined up for the refresher clinic on April 19, 2025. We just sourced a clean 2018 Ram 1500 Quad Cab, and I asked our yard guys to cut the right B‑pillar and rear cab corner specifically with the demo in mind. I told them to avoid torch heat near the outer skin and to leave the inner reinforcements attached so we can show the students how the structure actually ties together instead of looking at a bare skin.

My plan is to bring that single demo cab section into the Hawkeye Community College collision lab the morning of the I‑CAR refresher. I figured we could walk through locating the factory seams on the B‑pillar, sectioning the rear cab corner, and handling the roof rail without over‑cutting. I’m also having them leave a bit of extra roof and rear cab panel material on the passenger side so we’ve got room to adjust the section live without running out of metal.

Things at Waterloo Auto Parts have been busy – OSHA follow-up in the Truck Sectioning bay last month after one of our guys, Miguel, sliced his hand on a rushed Ram cut. HR scheduled me to do a remedial safety session on March 12 in our conference room, so this demo piece kind of kills two birds: I can practice the exact procedure I’m supposed to show my own crew and then bring the same mindset into your advanced collision repair module.

Let me know if there’s anything specific you want me to emphasize.

Jeremy Hansen
Recycled Truck Department
Waterloo Auto Parts

metadata.subject: Ram 1500 demo cab section for April 19 refresher

artifacts.content:
Megan: You good for Sunday chili at our place? Kids want to see Evie.

Jeremy: Yeah, sounds good. Just gotta juggle a few things. Feb and March are turning into doctor month.

Megan: What now??

Jeremy: Dad’s oncology check at MercyOne Waterloo Medical Center on Feb 27, 9:30 a.m. I’m taking him so Mom doesn’t have to stress. Trying to schedule that Ram 1500 Quad Cab cab sectioning job for that afternoon so I can stay close to home if his chemo knocks him on his butt again and Laura calls from W 8th Street.

Megan: Thanks for handling that. I can swing by their place after work if you’re stuck.

Jeremy: Might take you up on it. Then on March 3 I’ve got a 6:45 a.m. appointment at Allen Hospital to get my lower back checked again. Strain from wrestling that full Ram cab off the rack last winter is still bugging me. Dr. Stevens keeps telling me to stop trying to be 25.

Megan: LISTEN TO DR. STEVENS.

Jeremy: I’m trying. I told the yard to leave the rear cab panel cut a bit taller and band it solid to the pallet so I can just grab it with the forklift when I roll in late that morning. No more sketchy solo lifts.

Megan: Between Dad’s chemo, your back, and Mom’s blood pressure, we’re falling apart lol.

Jeremy: Yep. At least Caleb’s just got basketball and Nora’s piano to worry about.

Megan: And you still dreaming about that little fishing boat.

Jeremy: Always.

Megan: Sunday 3:00? I’ll bring cornbread.

Jeremy: Perfect.

Tuesday nights – spring 2025

Things to remember:

1) Parenting class
- Court-ordered parenting class at the Black Hawk County Family Services building on Tuesday nights this spring, running through May 2025.
- Starts at 6:00 p.m., usually out around 7:15–7:30 p.m.
- This whole custody mess with Emily over Caleb and Nora is wearing me out, but I need to show up, listen, and not treat it like a box to check.

2) Work schedule around it
- Plan to be back at the shop after 7:30 p.m. on Tuesdays if I’m going to work late.
- Ask the yard to keep cuts straight and give me at least 4 inches of extra roof and rear cab panel metal on the passenger side on that 2018 Ram 1500 Quad Cab job. I’ll need room to adjust the section quietly when I’m the only one in the bay.
- Keep noise down – no torching after 9 p.m., just saw cuts and layout.

3) Safety / training tie-in
- Still thinking about the OSHA follow-up in the Truck Sectioning bay and Miguel’s hand injury. Use these late-night sessions to slow down, practice the exact procedure I want to show the guys at the March 12, 2025 remedial safety training in the conference room.
- Leave 4–5 inches of extra material around the B‑pillar/rocker when possible so I’ve got a visual to walk them through.

4) Personal reminders
- Eat something before the parenting class so I’m not a zombie.
- Text Emily about pickup/drop-off for Caleb’s basketball and Nora’s piano if the schedule shifts.
- Don’t stay past 10:00 p.m. in the shop – back won’t forgive it the next morning.

metadata.title: Tuesday night plan – parenting class + Ram 1500 work

artifacts.content:
Event: Allen Hospital – back follow-up (Dr. Stevens)
Date: Monday, March 3, 2025
Time: 6:45–8:30 a.m.
Location: Allen Hospital, Waterloo – outpatient clinic
Attendees: Jeremy

Description:
Lower back check-up after the strain from wrestling that full Ram cab off the rack last winter at Waterloo Auto Parts. Dr. Stevens wants to see if the exercises are helping and make sure I’m not doing anything stupid at work.

To bring/remember:
- List of what still hurts (climbing into high trucks, twisting while carrying doors, etc.).
- Ask specifically about limits on lifting cab sections and how often I can be on concrete before my back pays for it.

Event: Unload Ram 1500 right rear cab section
Date: Monday, March 3, 2025
Time: 10:00–11:00 a.m.
Location: Waterloo Auto Parts – Truck Sectioning bay / yard
Attendees: Jeremy

Description:
Yard will have the 2018 Ram 1500 right rear cab corner / rear cab panel section waiting on a pallet. I asked them to leave the rear cab panel cut a bit taller than usual (down to just below midpoint of the rear glass opening) and band it solidly to the pallet so I can unload it by myself safely when I get back from Allen.

Notes:
- Use forklift only – no manhandling the section.
- If back is barking after the appointment, ask Miguel or one of the other guys to spot.
- Keep in mind upcoming April 19, 2025 Hawkeye CC I‑CAR refresher with Dan Miller – this piece might double as a backup demo if the first Ram section doesn’t pan out.
